2026-04-17T08:30:52.5224069Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T08:30:52.5224491Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T08:30:52.5224859Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T08:30:52.5225147Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T08:30:52.5225378Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-17T08:30:52.5226860Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. 2026-04-17T08:34:16.8291982Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T08:31:59.091882226Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58273,\"title\":\"[CognitiveServices] Docs: Document new resource types added in TypeSpec migration (v1.6.0-beta.1)\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/`\\n**Triggered by:** Commit [REDACTED] (PR #57069) by `@live1206`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe package was migrated from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec, upgrading the API version to 2026-03-01. This added several new custom resource types and supporting models:\\n\\n- `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` — manage AI Foundry Projects under a Cognitive Services account\\n- `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostCollection` / `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` — manage capability hosts scoped to a project\\n- `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostData` — data model shared by account- and project-scoped capability hosts\\n- `CognitiveServicesConnectionProperties` / `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostProperties` — new model types\\n- `CognitiveServicesExtensions` — new extension methods on `ArmClient` and `SubscriptionResource`\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **README \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section is empty of service content** — it links to the generic Azure SDK docs page but does not describe any of the resource types in this package (accounts, projects, capability hosts, connections, deployments, etc.)\\n- **README \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section has no inline code snippets** — it only links to `(aka.ms/redacted) The `tests/Samples/` folder contains 35+ generated sample files (including new ones for `CognitiveServicesProject`, `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHost`, `CognitiveServicesConnection`) but none are wired into the README via `Snippet:` placeholders\\n- **CHANGELOG `1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` is incomplete** — only records \\u0026#34;Upgraded api-version to 2026-03-01\\u0026#34; without listing the new resource types (`CognitiveServicesProject`, `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHost`, capability host data models, new extension methods) added by the migration\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### 1a. Update `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section to enumerate new resource types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Upgraded api-version to 2026-03-01.\\n- Added `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` to manage AI Foundry Projects nested under a Cognitive Services account.\\n- Added `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` and `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostCollection` to manage capability hosts scoped to a project.\\n- Added `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostData` as the shared data model for both account-level and project-level capability hosts.\\n- Added `CognitiveServicesConnectionProperties` and `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostProperties` model types.\\n- Added `GetCognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` extension method on `ArmClient`.\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n\\n### Bugs Fixed\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Edit `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the generic **Key concepts** section with service-specific content, and add `Snippet:` placeholders to the **Examples** section. See Step 2 below for the snippet details.\\n\\nReplace the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section body:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### CognitiveServicesAccountResource\\n\\nRepresents a top-level Azure Cognitive Services account. Accounts hold AI models, deployments, connections, projects, and capability hosts.\\n\\n### CognitiveServicesAccountDeploymentResource\\n\\nRepresents a model deployment within an account. Use this to create, update, or delete deployments of AI models.\\n\\n### CognitiveServicesProjectResource\\n\\nRepresents an AI Foundry Project nested within a Cognitive Services account. Projects can have their own connections and capability hosts.\\n\\n### CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostResource / CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource\\n\\nRepresents a capability host attached either to an account or to a project. Capability hosts define the AI capabilities (e.g., storage, vector search) available to the parent resource.\\n\\n### Thread safety\\n\\nWe guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline]((azure.github.io/redacted) This ensures that the recommendation of reusing client instances is always safe, even across threads.\\n\\n### Additional concepts\\n\\n[Client options](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#configuring-service-clients-using-clientoptions) |\\n[Accessing the response](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#accessing-http-response-details-using-responset) |\\n[Long-running operations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#consuming-long-running-operations-using-operationt) |\\n[Handling failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#reporting-errors-requestfailedexception) |\\n[Diagnostics](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/samples/Diagnostics.md) |\\n[Mocking]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) |\\n[Client lifetime]((devblogs.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n\\n```\\n\\nReplace the \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section body:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples show common operations using the management library.\\n\\n### List all Cognitive Services accounts in a subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create or update an account\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a project within an account\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe repository uses a snippet-generation tool: write sample code in a test file with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`, add a placeholder in the README, then run `Update-Snippets.ps1` to inject the code.\\n\\n#### Snippet 1 — List accounts: `CognitiveServices_GetAccounts`\\n\\n**Test file to create:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/tests/Samples/Sample_ReadmeSnippets.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation.                Console.WriteLine(account.Data.Name);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            CognitiveServicesAccountCollection accounts = resourceGroup.GetCognitiveServicesAccounts();\\n            CognitiveServicesAccountData accountData = new CognitiveServicesAccountData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                Kind = \\u0026#34;OpenAI\\u0026#34;,\\n                Sku = new Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Models.CognitiveServicesSku(\\u0026#34;S0\\u0026#34;),\\n            };\\n            ArmOperation operation = await accounts.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myAccount\\u0026#34;, accountData);\\n            CognitiveServicesAccountResource account = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created account: {account.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateProjectAsync()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateProject\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n 2026-04-17T08:34:16.8382698Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T08:31:59.378884047Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58273,\"title\":\"[CognitiveServices] Docs: Document new resource types added in TypeSpec migration (v1.6.0-beta.1)\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/`\\n**Triggered by:** Commit [REDACTED] (PR #57069) by `@live1206`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nThe package was migrated from AutoRest/Swagger to TypeSpec, upgrading the API version to 2026-03-01. This added several new custom resource types and supporting models:\\n\\n- `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` — manage AI Foundry Projects under a Cognitive Services account\\n- `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostCollection` / `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` — manage capability hosts scoped to a project\\n- `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostData` — data model shared by account- and project-scoped capability hosts\\n- `CognitiveServicesConnectionProperties` / `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostProperties` — new model types\\n- `CognitiveServicesExtensions` — new extension methods on `ArmClient` and `SubscriptionResource`\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **README \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section is empty of service content** — it links to the generic Azure SDK docs page but does not describe any of the resource types in this package (accounts, projects, capability hosts, connections, deployments, etc.)\\n- **README \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section has no inline code snippets** — it only links to `(aka.ms/redacted) The `tests/Samples/` folder contains 35+ generated sample files (including new ones for `CognitiveServicesProject`, `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHost`, `CognitiveServicesConnection`) but none are wired into the README via `Snippet:` placeholders\\n- **CHANGELOG `1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` is incomplete** — only records \\u0026#34;Upgraded api-version to 2026-03-01\\u0026#34; without listing the new resource types (`CognitiveServicesProject`, `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHost`, capability host data models, new extension methods) added by the migration\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### 1a. Update `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/CHANGELOG.md`\\n\\nEdit the `1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` section to enumerate new resource types:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## 1.6.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Upgraded api-version to 2026-03-01.\\n- Added `CognitiveServicesProjectResource` to manage AI Foundry Projects nested under a Cognitive Services account.\\n- Added `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` and `CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostCollection` to manage capability hosts scoped to a project.\\n- Added `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostData` as the shared data model for both account-level and project-level capability hosts.\\n- Added `CognitiveServicesConnectionProperties` and `CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostProperties` model types.\\n- Added `GetCognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource` extension method on `ArmClient`.\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n\\n### Bugs Fixed\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Edit `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the generic **Key concepts** section with service-specific content, and add `Snippet:` placeholders to the **Examples** section. See Step 2 below for the snippet details.\\n\\nReplace the \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section body:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n### CognitiveServicesAccountResource\\n\\nRepresents a top-level Azure Cognitive Services account. Accounts hold AI models, deployments, connections, projects, and capability hosts.\\n\\n### CognitiveServicesAccountDeploymentResource\\n\\nRepresents a model deployment within an account. Use this to create, update, or delete deployments of AI models.\\n\\n### CognitiveServicesProjectResource\\n\\nRepresents an AI Foundry Project nested within a Cognitive Services account. Projects can have their own connections and capability hosts.\\n\\n### CognitiveServicesCapabilityHostResource / CognitiveServicesProjectCapabilityHostResource\\n\\nRepresents a capability host attached either to an account or to a project. Capability hosts define the AI capabilities (e.g., storage, vector search) available to the parent resource.\\n\\n### Thread safety\\n\\nWe guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline]((azure.github.io/redacted) This ensures that the recommendation of reusing client instances is always safe, even across threads.\\n\\n### Additional concepts\\n\\n[Client options](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#configuring-service-clients-using-clientoptions) |\\n[Accessing the response](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#accessing-http-response-details-using-responset) |\\n[Long-running operations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#consuming-long-running-operations-using-operationt) |\\n[Handling failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#reporting-errors-requestfailedexception) |\\n[Diagnostics](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/samples/Diagnostics.md) |\\n[Mocking]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) |\\n[Client lifetime]((devblogs.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n\\n```\\n\\nReplace the \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section body:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following examples show common operations using the management library.\\n\\n### List all Cognitive Services accounts in a subscription\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create or update an account\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Create a project within an account\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe repository uses a snippet-generation tool: write sample code in a test file with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`, add a placeholder in the README, then run `Update-Snippets.ps1` to inject the code.\\n\\n#### Snippet 1 — List accounts: `CognitiveServices_GetAccounts`\\n\\n**Test file to create:** `sdk/cognitiveservices/Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices/tests/Samples/Sample_ReadmeSnippets.cs`\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Samples\\n{\\n    public partial class Sample_ReadmeSnippets\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task GetAccountsAsync()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_GetAccounts\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            await foreach (CognitiveServicesAccountResource account in subscription.GetCognitiveServicesAccountsAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine(account.Data.Name);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateAccountAsync()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateAccount\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            CognitiveServicesAccountCollection accounts = resourceGroup.GetCognitiveServicesAccounts();\\n            CognitiveServicesAccountData accountData = new CognitiveServicesAccountData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;))\\n            {\\n                Kind = \\u0026#34;OpenAI\\u0026#34;,\\n                Sku = new Azure.ResourceManager.CognitiveServices.Models.CognitiveServicesSku(\\u0026#34;S0\\u0026#34;),\\n            };\\n            ArmOperation operation = await accounts.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myAccount\\u0026#34;, accountData);\\n            CognitiveServicesAccountResource account = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created account: {account.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only validating compilation of examples\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateProjectAsync()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CognitiveServices_CreateProject\\n            TokenCredential cred = new DefaultAzureCredential();\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(cred);\\n\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(\\u0026#34;myResourceGroup\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            CognitiveServicesAccountResource account = await resourceGroup\\n                .GetCognitiveServicesAccountAsync(\\u0026#34;myAccount\\u0026#34;);\\n\\n            CognitiveServicesProjectCollection projects = account.GetCognitiveServicesProjects();\\n            CognitiveServicesProjectData projectData = new CognitiveServicesProjectData(\\n                new Azure.Core.AzureLocation(\\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;));\\n            ArmOperation operation = await projects.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                Azure.WaitUntil.Completed, \\u0026#34;myProject\\u0026#34;, projectData);\\n            CognitiveServicesProjectResource project = operation.Value;\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created project: {project.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**README placeholders** — the three `Snippet:` fences added in Step 1b will be filled in by
